quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 2
quiz.subtitle: Theorems Regarding Sides, Angles, and Diagonals
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: A parallelogram is defined as a quadrilateral with:
options: Both pairs of opposite sides parallel
Exactly one pair of parallel sides
questions.explanation: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral in which both pairs of opposite sides are parallel. This is the defining property from which all other properties follow.

questions.question: In a parallelogram, which statement about opposite sides is true?
options: Opposite sides are both parallel and congruent
Opposite sides are parallel but not necessarily congruent
Opposite sides are congruent but not necessarily parallel
questions.explanation: In any parallelogram, opposite sides are both parallel (by definition) and congruent (by the Opposite Sides Theorem).

questions.question: In a parallelogram, which statement about opposite angles is true?
questions.explanation: In any parallelogram, opposite angles are congruent. Consecutive angles are supplementary (sum to $180^\circ$), but opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: In a parallelogram, which statement about consecutive angles is true?
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles of a parallelogram are supplementary — their measures sum to $180^\circ$. This follows from the fact that parallel lines cut by a transversal create same-side interior angles.

questions.question: In a parallelogram, which statement about the diagonals is true?
questions.explanation: In any parallelogram, the diagonals bisect each other — they intersect at their midpoints. Congruent diagonals, perpendicular diagonals, and angle-bisecting diagonals are properties of special parallelograms.

questions.question: In parallelogram $ABCD$, $m\angle A = 72^\circ$. What is $m\angle C$?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles of a parallelogram are congruent. Since $\angle A$ and $\angle C$ are opposite, $m\angle C = m\angle A = 72^\circ$.

questions.question: In parallelogram $PQRS$, $m\angle P = 65^\circ$. What is $m\angle Q$?
questions.explanation: $\angle P$ and $\angle Q$ are consecutive angles. Consecutive angles of a parallelogram are supplementary: $m\angle Q = 180^\circ - 65^\circ = 115^\circ$.

questions.question: In parallelogram $ABCD$, diagonals $\overline{AC}$ and $\overline{BD}$ intersect at $E$. If $AE = 7$, what is $AC$?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other, so $E$ is the midpoint of both diagonals. Since $AE = 7$, the full diagonal $AC = 2 \times 7 = 14$.

questions.question: In parallelogram $WXYZ$, $WX = 3x - 5$ and $YZ = 2x + 9$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: Opposite sides of a parallelogram are congruent. So $WX = YZ$: $3x - 5 = 2x + 9$, giving $x = 14$.

questions.question: A quadrilateral has diagonals that bisect each other. What must the quadrilateral be?
questions.explanation: If the diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other, the quadrilateral must be a parallelogram. This is one of the ways to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered all properties of parallelograms including opposite sides, opposite angles, consecutive angles, and diagonals.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main properties but review the difference between opposite angles (congruent) and consecutive angles (supplementary).
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: opposite sides are parallel and congruent, opposite angles are congruent, consecutive angles are supplementary, and diagonals bisect each other.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A parallelogram has: both pairs of opposite sides parallel and congruent, opposite angles congruent, consecutive angles supplementary, and diagonals that bisect each other.
